page: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external block storage attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in the Passmark memory score and strong performance in cached memory bandwidth, reaching 4138.93 GB/sec for small blocks. Multi-core CPU performance is also strong, as evidenced by high scores in stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests. However, uncached large-block memory read and write speeds (122.1 GB/sec and 62.34 GB/sec respectively) fall into the average category. Cryptographic and integer operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions such as AES, VAES, and SHA-NI.

While the instance excels at memory-intensive tasks and database operations, showing strong results in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, it exhibits clear performance tradeoffs in large-scale machine learning. LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing and text generation for small 135M models, but text generation speed drops to a weak 2.75 tokens/sec when running a large 70B model. Given these characteristics, this server is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and medium-scale data processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing, exceptional cached memory bandwidth, and highly efficient database operations, though uncached memory bandwidth remains average. A notable tradeoff is its weak text generation speed for large 70B LLM models, contrasting with strong performance on smaller models. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caches like Redis, and enterprise-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage support, designed for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, built for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs and 512 GB of system memory.
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB RAM per vCPU.
Strong performance in database operations and Redis caching.
Premium Storage capable for high-performance external block storage.
Suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications and relational databases.
